blocks.body: A five-and-a-half-year medical degree (including a one-year rotating internship) that qualifies you as a doctor. Most graduates then specialise via NEET-PG (MD/MS), or work in clinical practice, government health services or research.

blocks.title: How you get in
blocks.body: NEET-UG — the single national entrance exam for all MBBS seats.

blocks.title: At a glance
items: Highly competitive: lakhs of applicants for ~1.1 lakh seats.
Most doctors specialise later via NEET-PG (MD/MS).
Paths include clinical practice, government service, study abroad and research.
